description: Prompt assembly varies across runs for identical inputs because of timestamps, random ordering, or unstable iteration.
body: Failure mode: the assembled prompt differs run to run for the same inputs because a timestamp, random id, or map-iteration order leaks into it, so evaluation is unreproducible and regressions hide in the noise. Detection signals: clock or random values interpolated; unsorted map/set iteration in assembly; snapshot tests flaky or absent. Do not let nondeterministic values into assembly. Safer fallback: sort keys and lists deterministically, isolate genuinely dynamic values behind explicit parameters, freeze the template, and add a snapshot test that identical inputs produce a byte-identical prompt.
